title: Slssk
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Youmay|the [[Hutlarian]] bounty hunter Slssk|the similarly-named [[Barabel/Legends|Barabel]] bounty hunter, [[Slizzik]]}}

{{Quote|Ten years as a gladiator on Loovria didn't kill my first mate. What makes you think this target stands a chance?|Beilert Valance|<ref name="valance2" />}}
'''Slssk''' was a [[Hutlarian]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served as the [[First Mate/Legends|first mate]] on former [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] [[Stormtrooper Commander/Legends|commander]] [[Beilert Valance/Legends|Beilert Valance's]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] crew during the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Noted for his martial prowess even among his warlike [[Species/Legends|species]], Slssk cut his [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]] as a [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiator]] on [[Loovria/Legends|Loovria]], garnering a fearsome reputation. Eventually, he hooked up with Valance, participating in the hunter's brutal [[Attack on Anglebay Station|annihilation]] of [[Anglebay Station]], an unarmed and neutral medical station on [[Telos IV]]. Afterward, Slssk followed Valance to [[Aduba-3/Legends|Aduba-3]] as part of Valance's quest to track down a mysterious "[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]" who had become the object of his utter obsession. There, they blundered into a trap set by the [[Lepi/Legends|Lepi]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Jaxxon/Legends|Jaxxon]], and Slssk was shot dead by the Lepi's partner, [[Amaiza Foxtrain/Legends|Amaiza Foxtrain]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|We're rounding up all the robot orderlies, captain.|Slssk to Beilert Valance|<ref name="marvel16" />}}
[[File:CoolGuySlssk.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Slssk at his post aboard the ''Kill Switch''.]]
A [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Hutlarian]], Slssk was a native of the planet [[Hutlar]], located in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] [[Senex sector/Legends|Senex sector]]. Although Hutlarians were notorious for their toughness, but during the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Slssk became a legend even among his own kind. For ten [[Standard year/Legends|years]], Slssk survived uncommon brutality as a [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiator]] on [[Loovria/Legends|Loovria]], covering his [[Color/Legends|orange]] skin with scars in the process. After his [[Standard day/Legends|days]] as a gladiator ended, Slssk went into [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunting]], joining the crew of unhinged former [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] [[Stormtrooper Commander/Legends|commander]] [[Beilert Valance/Legends|Beilert Valance]]. Slssk's reputation preceded him—in certain circles, he was nearly as feared as Valance himself—and the Hutlarian became Valance's [[First Mate/Legends|first mate]], serving aboard the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] ''[[Kill Switch]]''.<ref name="valance2" />

However, in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], things took a dark turn for Valance's crew. Just a few weeks after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Valance told his gang that he had a new mission, one that he would pay for himself: completely [[Attack on Anglebay Station|annihilate]] [[Anglebay Station]],<ref name="valance2" /> a neutral, unarmed and defenseless medical station on [[Telos IV]].<ref name="marvel16" /> Although many of even the most hardened members of the crew balked at Valance's plan,<ref name="valance2" /> the mission continued nonetheless, and Slssk joined Valance in storming the hospital and massacring patients and staff. Valance, who was partially driven by a roiling hatred of [[Droid/Legends|droids]], specifically directed Slssk to round up and destroy the station's [[Unidentified robot orderly|robot orderlies]]. Slssk and the crew quickly destroyed the station, but not before coming across a piece of chatter connecting a<ref name="marvel16" /> [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]]-based<ref name="tnms">{{SW|url=news/the-not-so-magnificent-seven|text=The Not-So Magnificent Seven|int=The Not-So Magnificent Seven|nolive=1}}</ref> [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] named [[Jaxxon/Legends|Jaxxon]] to an unnamed "[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|farmboy]]" wanted by the Empire in relation to the [[Rescue of Princess Leia/Legends|escape]] of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaanian]] [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] from Imperial captivity.<ref name="marvel16" />

Valance had become utterly obsessed with the farmboy in the aftermath of the Battle of Yavin, and ordered Slssk to focus all his efforts on learning as much as he could about Jaxxon and the connection to the farmboy. That trail led the ''Kill Switch'' to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Farm/Legends|farmworld]] of [[Aduba-3/Legends|Aduba-3]], where they believed Jaxxon, [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] hero [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and a [[Jimm Doshun|character]] they believed to be Valance's farmboy had teamed up to defend a small [[Onacra|farming village]] from a [[Cloud-Riders/Legends|swoop gang]]. The ''Kill Switch'' arrived to find that Jaxxon and his partner, [[Amaiza Foxtrain/Legends|Amaiza Foxtrain]], had preceded them there. Jaxxon made a run on the ''Kill Switch'' with his ship, the [[Rabbit's Foot/Legends|''Rabbit's Foot'']], only to be blasted down to the planet's surface. Slssk and the gang gave chase, only to find that Jaxxon, Foxtrain and the local villagers had taken up position in the nearby fields. It was then that Slssk and his compatriots realized that they had blundered right into a trap: the villagers stampeded their [[Bantha/Legends|banthas]], massive beasts who violently trampled Valance's gang. Many were killed, but Slssk managed to survive the stampede. A fighter to the end, Slssk attempted to rejoin the battle and attack his enemies, but was swiftly shot and killed by the quick-triggered Foxtrain.<ref name="marvel16" />

==Personality and traits==
Uncommonly tough even among members of his [[Species/Legends|species]], Slssk's warlike reputation and his pockmarked body evoked an intimidating image. In fact, Valance used Slssk as an avatar for his crew's martial prowess: if someone asked whether his gang was up for taking on a certain enemy, Valance had Slssk remove his [[battle armor]], using his first mate's scarred torso and his history as a gladiator on Loovria to answer in the affirmative. Although many of Valance's men were unsettled by the violent missions he dispatched them on,<ref name="valance2" /> Slssk stayed loyal to Valance until the end, doing the hunter's bidding until it caused his death.<ref name="marvel16" />

==Behind the scenes==
Slssk made his first appearance in [[Star Wars (1977) 16|''Star Wars'' (1977) 16]], written by [[Archie Goodwin]] and released in [[1978]]. The Hutlarian's name was given as both "Slssk" and "Slzzk" in different panels of the comic,<ref name="marvel16" /> and was definitively established as "Slssk" in ''[[The Hunt Within: Valance's Tale]]'', a set of [[Star Wars Miniatures|''Star Wars'' Miniatures]] scenarios released between [[2004]] and [[2005]].<ref name="valance2" /> ''The Hunt Within'' greatly expands on Slssk's background and species, with this new information coming as an invention of author [[Jason Fry]].<ref name="valance5">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=swminis/article/huntwithin5|text=The Hunt Within: Valance's Tale: Conclusion|int=The Hunt Within: Valance's Tale: Conclusion}}</ref> During his debut appearance in the [[Star Wars (1977)|Marvel ''Star Wars'']] comics, Slssk was illustrated by [[Walter Simonson|Walt Simonson]].
